findings.evidence: `rg -n "Set.range \(cmRoot p\)|cmRoot"` shows the range equality only as local `have hrange` at lines 129-130, while the public equivalence at lines 98-100 uses `adjoin ℚ (Set.range (cmRoot p))`.
findings.fix: Add a public simp/characteristic lemma for `Set.range (cmRoot p)` equaling the inserted generator set, or state/derive the Galois equivalence directly over the inserted-field type.
findings.issue: `galoisGroupEquivISqrtPrimes` is stated for `adjoin ℚ (Set.range (cmRoot p))`, but the named downstream field/API is `adjoin ℚ (insert Complex.I (Set.range ...))`; the only bridge `Set.range (cmRoot p) = insert ...` is a local proof inside `card_aut_adjoin_I_sqrt_primes`, so consumers cannot use the isomorphism at the advertised field without unfolding/reproving it.

findings.evidence: Lines 54 and 58 mark the helper definitions `@[expose]`; lines 61-67 provide the normal-form simp lemmas and line 71 provides `cmRoot_sq`, so consumers should not need body unfolding.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from these helper definitions and rely on `cmRadicand_none/some`, `cmRoot_none/some`, `cmRoot_sq`, and the requested range lemma for public use.
findings.issue: `cmRadicand` and `cmRoot` are marked `@[expose]`, exposing implementation bodies even though the file already provides the needed pointwise simp lemmas and characteristic theorem.

summary: The PR exposes the right core result, but the public interface is not yet usable at the named field without unfolding helpers, and it exposes helper bodies unnecessarily.
